executionSpine.name: Execution Spine
executionSpine.tagline: The layer that carries work from signal to closure.
executionSpine.systemRole: The controlled execution-carrying layer that sits across the Applied Intelligence ecosystem and ensures important work does not get lost between detection, routing, ownership, action, escalation, and closure.
executionSpine.corePrinciple: Nothing important gets lost between people, departments, or decisions.

purpose.summary: Convert meaningful signals into managed execution.
endStates: intentionally parked with clear ownership and reason
escalated into a higher decision path
purpose.strategicOutcome: Turns Applied Intelligence from a collection of smart modules into a true operational system.

whyItMatters.commonSystemWeakness: Very few systems reliably carry work from first signal to verified closure.
whyItMatters.claim: The Execution Spine owns the gap between detection and closure so the system does not become a passive reporting environment.

coreDefinition.summary: The universal work-carrying layer for Applied Intelligence.
coreDefinition.signalToObjectRule: A meaningful issue, request, risk, action, or coordination need enters the system as a signal and is converted into a live execution object.
coreDefinition.result: Once converted, the item is no longer treated as static information. It becomes managed work.
signalExamples: trace pattern requiring visibility or action

isNot: a replacement for root cause analysis
a replacement for continuous improvement logic
a replacement for case study storytelling

operatingRule.goldenRule: If it matters enough to track, it matters enough to carry.

executionObjectModel.purpose: The live record that carries meaningful work through the system.

lifecycle.name: Signal
lifecycle.description: A meaningful issue, request, risk, or action enters the system.

lifecycle.name: Conversion
lifecycle.description: The signal is converted into an Execution Object.

lifecycle.name: Routing
lifecycle.description: The object is routed to the correct owner, department, and visibility group.

lifecycle.name: Active Work
lifecycle.description: The item is being worked, reviewed, coordinated, or resolved.

lifecycle.name: Cross-Department Movement
lifecycle.description: The same object expands through linked departments when required.

lifecycle.name: Escalation
lifecycle.description: If action stalls, risk increases, or timing fails, escalation rules trigger.

lifecycle.name: Verification
lifecycle.description: Required closure proof or signoff is checked.

lifecycle.name: Closure
lifecycle.description: The object is closed with visible disposition and verification data.

lifecycle.name: Reopen if Needed
lifecycle.description: If closure fails or the problem returns, the object is reopened under controlled rules.

agentRelationshipModel.summary: The Execution Spine does not replace Applied Intelligence agents. It gives them a controlled path into real execution.

ai-trace.spineUse: Can push execution-worthy items into the spine.

ai-cis.spineUse: Can push owned follow-through work into the spine.

role: manages containment and corrective action flow
ai-rma.spineUse: Uses the spine for routing, ownership, escalation, and closure visibility.

ai-roi.spineUse: Can generate approval-path execution candidates when action is required.

role: surfaces quoting, fixturing, and production-flow risk
ai-quote.spineUse: Can push actionable risks into the spine when cross-functional work is required.

role: tells validated stories after the work is done
ai-case.spineUse: Can reference closed spine objects as story sources later.

role: acts as the primary visible movement layer
ai-connect.spineUse: Should be the main user-facing behavior layer through which the Execution Spine is experienced.

role: formats outputs linked to spine objects

agentRelationshipModel.bestRelationshipStatement: AI-Connect is the visible movement layer. Execution Spine is the controlled work-carrying layer underneath it.

corePrinciples: Context must travel with the work.

examples: A welding issue with delivery risk routes to the department owner first, then planning or leadership if overdue.
An equipment failure routes to the responsible department, support owner, and elevated visibility if downtime exceeds threshold.
A meeting action item routes to the named owner and reappears before the next meeting if unresolved.
An RMA-related issue routes to quality, containment owner, and leadership visibility when customer exposure is confirmed.

closureStandard.coreRule: Closure is a controlled state, not a casual opinion.

closureProofExamples.processIssue: routing, instruction, or control updated and verified
closureProofExamples.roiCandidate: approved, rejected, or deferred with visible owner and reason
closureProofExamples.tracePattern: linked disposition into CIS, RMA, monitoring, or hold status

closureStandard.purpose: Protects the system from fake completion and cosmetic closure.

triggers: closure proof is missing or invalid
customer return or external feedback reopens exposure
trace data shows the same problem recurring
follow-up review proves no real correction occurred

productImplications.summary: The Execution Spine should shape the structure of the Applied Intelligence app experience.

laneInterpretation.Handoffs: execution objects changing owner or department
laneInterpretation.Threads / Communication: communication attached to live execution objects
laneInterpretation.Meetings / Prep: execution objects requiring alignment, prep, or review

productImplications.claim: These are visible slices of the same underlying execution system, not disconnected features.

phasedBuildDirection.name: Ownership and movement
phasedBuildDirection.purpose: Make work visible, routed, and owned.

phasedBuildDirection.name: Cross-agent integration
phasedBuildDirection.purpose: Make the ecosystem operate as one system.
includes: dashboard links back to live objects

phasedBuildDirection.name: Executive operating layer
phasedBuildDirection.purpose: Turn the spine into an operations command backbone.

avoid: detached records with no routing relationship
chat messages treated as execution by themselves
systems that look smart but do not carry work
dataModelPhilosophy.designGoal: Fast to create and strong enough to manage real operational follow-through.

eventHistory.purpose: Creates operational memory, improves handoffs, and supports accountability without noise.

permissionsModel.keyRule: Visibility should expand with responsibility, not with noise.

visibilityLevels.Standard User: own work and directly linked items
visibilityLevels.President / Executive: major risk, overdue escalation, and cross-functional exposure

strategicValue.summary: One of the strongest moat-creating concepts inside Applied Intelligence.
strategicValue.fewSystemsCanHonestlyClaim: We carry important work from first signal to verified closure across departments.
whyHardToReplace: recurring failure becomes harder to hide

positioning.plainEnglishDescription: The operating layer that carries important work through the system until it is truly closed.
positioning.expandedBrandLine: The Execution Spine carries work from signal to closure so nothing important gets lost, stalled, or forgotten.

finalSystemStatement.summary: If Applied Intelligence is going to become more than a collection of smart modules, it needs a core layer that guarantees movement, ownership, escalation, and closure.
finalSystemStatement.endState: Applied Intelligence becomes the system that does not let critical work die in the gap.

repoPlacementRecommendation.masterSourceIntegration: Add this object at the top level of the Applied Intelligence master source file under the key executionSpine.
repoPlacementRecommendation.derivedBundleExpectation: App and website source bundles should inherit the relevant executionSpine fields from the master source.
